cv_banned_phrases.phrase: take it to the next level

cv_openers.example: At Safaricom, rebuilt the billing pipeline.
After the migration, cut costs by 30%.
When traffic spiked, scaled the cluster cleanly.
During the 2024 rollout, trained 14 field agents.
Working with engineering, reduced latency by 40%.
Leading a 6-person squad, delivered the rewrite.
On behalf of ops, negotiated SLAs with vendors.

cv_result_connectors.example: shaving 90 minutes off the close cycle
clearing the Q3 goal 6 weeks early

cv_result_connectors.connector: and held that gain for {n} quarters
cv_result_connectors.example: and held that gain for 4 quarters

cv_result_connectors.connector: on roughly {x} of the addressable base
cv_result_connectors.example: on roughly two-thirds of the addressable base

cv_sentence_structures.example: Cut report turnaround from 4 days to 6 hours.
Within 90 days, retired the legacy ETL.
After the merger, consolidated 3 CRMs into one, cutting licence spend by $120k/yr.
Rebuilt the onboarding flow with the design team, lifting activation from 41% to 67% in two quarters.
Closed it in two weeks — a record for the team.
Migrated 14 services to k8s (no rollback needed).

cv_voice_profiles.name: storyteller_executive
cv_voice_profiles.description: Reads like an article — context, conflict, result.

cv_voice_profiles.name: numbers_first
cv_voice_profiles.description: Almost every bullet starts with or pivots on a number.

cv_voice_profiles.name: craftsperson
cv_voice_profiles.description: Quiet pride in the work; specific tools and choices.

cv_voice_profiles.name: public_servant
cv_voice_profiles.description: Plain, accountable language for public-sector work.

cv_voice_profiles.name: field_practitioner
cv_voice_profiles.description: Specific places, named programmes, real units.
